name: Known-item vs exploratory paths
logic: Two buyers hit your catalogue: the repeat customer who knows the part number and wants to type 'VPC-DWH-12' and go, and the new buyer who needs to browse and filter their way to the right family. Serve both — a part-number search box for the known-item buyer, faceted browse for the explorer — and never make one wear the other's shoes. NN/g's e-commerce search research shows search and faceted navigation must support both narrowing to a known item and open-ended exploration; conflating them frustrates everyone.

evidence.title: E-Commerce UX: Search, incl. Faceted Search (Report)
evidence.note: NN/g: search + faceted navigation must serve both known-item lookups and exploratory browsing; the two behaviours need different affordances.

exemplars.name: McMaster-Carr
exemplars.why: A part-number search box AND deep parametric browse coexist — the reference for serving both buyer modes.

exemplars.name: Grainger
exemplars.why: MRO catalogue tuned for fast known-item reorder alongside category browse.
